If\\n173: // there is an overlap, returns an error. \ts.hdr.fill(resp.Header)\n52: \treturn resp, nil\n53: }\n54: \n55: func (s *kvServer) Put(ctx context.Context, r *pb.PutRequest) (*pb.PutResponse, error) {\n56: \tif err := checkPutRequest(r); err != \t\treturn nil, err\n86: \t}\n87: \t// check for forbidden put/del overlaps after checking request to avoid quadratic blowup\n88: \tif _, _, err := checkIntervals(r.Success); err != nil {\n89: \t\treturn nil, err\n90: \t}\n91: \tif _, _, err := checkIntervals(r.Failure); err != nil {\n92: \t\treturn nil, err\n93: \t}\n94: \n95: \tresp, err := s.kv.Txn(ctx, r)\n96: \tif err != nil {\n97: \treturn resp, nil\n112: }\n113: \n114: func checkRangeRequest(r *pb.RangeRequest) error {\n115: \tif len(r.Key) == 0 {\n116: \t\treturn rpctypes.ErrGRPCEmptyKey\n117: \t}\n118: \treturn nil\n119: }\n120: \n121: func checkPutRequest(r *pb.PutRequest) error {\n122: \tif len(r.Key) == 0 {\n123: \t\treturn rpctypes.ErrGRPCEmptyKey\n124: \t}\n125: \tif r.IgnoreValue && len(r.Value) != 0 {\n126: *pb.TxnRequest, maxTxnOps int) error {\n142: \topc := len(r.Compare)\n143: \tif opc < len(r.Success) {\n144: \t\topc = len(r.Success)\n145: \t}\n146: \tif opc < len(r.Failure) {\n147: \t\topc = len(r.Failure)\n148: \t}\n149: \tif opc > maxTxnOps {\n150: \t\treturn rpctypes.ErrGRPCTooManyOps\n151: \t}\n152: \n153: \tfor _, c := range r.Compare {\n154: \t\tif len(c.Key) == 0 {\n155: \t\t\treturn rpctypes.ErrGRPCEmptyKey\n156: \t\t}\n157: \t}\n158: \tfor _, u := range r.Success {\n159: \t\tif err := checkRequestOp(u, maxTxnOps-opc); err != nil {\n160: \t\t\treturn err\n161: \t\t}\n162: \t}\n163: \tfor _, u := range r.Failure {\n164: \t\tif err := checkRequestOp(u, maxTxnOps-opc); err != nil {\n165: \t\t\treturn err\n166: \t\t}\n167: \t}\n168: \n169: \treturn nil\n170: }\n171: \n172: // checkIntervals tests whether puts and deletes overlap for a list of ops. If\n173: // there is an overlap, returns an error. If no overlap, return put and delete\n174: // sets for recursive evaluation.\n175: func checkIntervals(reqs []*pb.RequestOp) (map[string]struct{}, adt.IntervalTree, error) {\n176: \tdels := adt.NewIntervalTree()\n177: \n178: \t// collect deletes from this level; build first to check lower level overlapped puts\n179: \tfor _, req := range reqs {\n180: \t\ttv, ok := req.Request.(*pb.RequestOp_RequestDeleteRange)\n181: \t\tif !ok {\n182: \t\t\tcontinue\n183: \t\t}\n184: \t\tdreq := tv.RequestDeleteRange\n185: \t\tif dreq == nil {\n186: \t\t\tcontinue\n187: \t\t}\n188: \t\tvar iv adt.Interval\n189: \t\tif len(dreq.RangeEnd) != 0 {\n190: \t\t\tiv = adt.NewStringAffineInterval(string(dreq.Key), string(dreq.RangeEnd))\n191: \t\t} else {\n192: \t\t\tiv = adt.NewStringAffinePoint(string(dreq.Key))\n193: \t\t}\n194: \t\tdels.Insert(iv, struct{}{})\n195: \t}\n196: \n197: \t// collect children puts/deletes\n198: \tputs := make(map[string]struct{})\n199: \tfor _, req := range reqs {\n200: \t\ttv, ok := req.Request.(*pb.RequestOp_RequestTxn)\n201: \t\tif !ok {\n202: \t\t\tcontinue\n203: \t\t}\n204: \t\tputsThen, delsThen, err := checkIntervals(tv.RequestTxn.Success)\n205: \t\tif err != nil {\n206: \t\t\treturn nil, dels, err\n207: \t\t}\n208: \t\tputsElse, delsElse, err := checkIntervals(tv.RequestTxn.Failure)\n209: \t\tif err != nil {\n210: \t\t\treturn nil, dels, err\n211: \t\t}\n212: \t\tfor k := range putsThen {\n213: \t\t\tif _, ok := puts[k]; ok {\n214: \t\t\t\treturn nil, dels, rpctypes.ErrGRPCDuplicateKey\n215: \t\t\t}\n216: \t\t\tif dels.Intersects(adt.NewStringAffinePoint(k)) {\n217: \t\t\t\treturn nil, dels, rpctypes.ErrGRPCDuplicateKey\n218: \t\t\t}\n219: \t\t\tputs[k] = struct{}{}\n220: \t\t}\n221: \t\tfor k := range putsElse {\n222: \t\t\tif _, ok := puts[k]; ok {\n223: \t\t\t\t// if key is from putsThen, overlap is OK since\n224: \t\t\t\t// either then/else are mutually exclusive\n225: \t\t\t\tif _, isSafe := putsThen[k]; !isSafe {\n226: \t\t\t\t\treturn nil, dels, rpctypes.ErrGRPCDuplicateKey\n227: \t\t\t\t}\n228: \t\t\t}\n229: \t\t\tif dels.Intersects(adt.NewStringAffinePoint(k)) {\n230: \t\t\t\treturn nil, dels, rpctypes.ErrGRPCDuplicateKey\n231: \t\t\t}\n232: \t\t\tputs[k] = struct{}{}\n233: \t\t}\n234: \t\tdels.Union(delsThen, adt.NewStringAffineInterval(\"\\x00\", \"\"))\n235: \t\tdels.Union(delsElse, adt.NewStringAffineInterval(\"\\x00\", \"\"))\n236: \t}\n237: \n238: \t// collect and check this level's puts\n239: \tfor _, req := range reqs {\n240: \t\ttv, ok := req.Request.(*pb.RequestOp_RequestPut)\n241: \t\tif !ok || tv.RequestPut == nil {\n242: \t\t\tcontinue\n243: \t\t}\n244: \t\tk := string(tv.RequestPut.Key)\n245: \t\tif _, ok := puts[k]; ok {\n246: \t\t\treturn nil, dels, rpctypes.ErrGRPCDuplicateKey\n247: \t\t}\n248: \t\tif dels.Intersects(adt.NewStringAffinePoint(k)) {\n249: \t\t\treturn nil, dels, rpctypes.ErrGRPCDuplicateKey\n250: \t\t}\n251: \t\tputs[k] = struct{}{}\n252: \t}\n253: \treturn puts, dels, nil\n254: }\n255: \n256: func checkRequestOp(u *pb.RequestOp, maxTxnOps int) error {\n257: \t// TODO: ensure only one of the field is set.\n258: \tswitch uv := u.Request.(type) {\n259: \tcase *pb.RequestOp_RequestRange:\n260: \t\treturn checkRangeRequest(uv.RequestRange)\n261: \tcase *pb.RequestOp_RequestPut:\n262: \t\treturn checkPutRequest(uv.RequestPut)\n263: \tcase *pb.RequestOp_RequestDeleteRange:\n264: \t\treturn checkDeleteRequest(uv.RequestDeleteRange)\n265: \tcase *pb.RequestOp_RequestTxn:\n266: \t\treturn checkTxnRequest(uv.RequestTxn, maxTxnOps)\n267: \tdefault:\n268: \t\t// empty op / nil entry\n269: \t\treturn rpctypes.ErrGRPCKeyNotFound\n270: \t}\n271: }\n272: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/etcd-io-etcd@17dae73b287c31f5cf3a70e48cf38a113a14f12a/-/blob/server/etcdserver/api/v3rpc/key.go"}}}

\\treturn resp.(*pb.LeaseRevokeResponse), nil\\n296: }\\n297: \\n298: func (s *EtcdServer) LeaseRenew(ctx context.Context, id lease.LeaseID) (int64, error) {\\n299: \\tif s.isLeader() {\\n300: \\t\\t// If s.isLeader() returns true, but we fail to ensure the current\\n301: \\t\\t// member's leadership, there are a couple of possibilities:\\n302: \\t\\t//   1. current member gets stuck on writing WAL entries;\\n303: \\t\\t//   2. current member is in network isolation status;\\n304: \\t\\t//   3. current member isn't a leader anymore (possibly due to #1 above).\\n305: \\t\\t// In such case, we just return error to client, so that the client can\\n306: \\t\\t// switch to another member to continue the lease keep-alive operation.\\n307: \\t\\tif !s.ensureLeadership() {\\n308: \\t\\t\\treturn -1, err\\n321: \\t\\t}\\n322: \\t}\\n323: \\n324: \\tcctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, s.Cfg.ReqTimeout())\\n325: \\tdefer cancel()\\n326: \\n327: \\t// renewals don't go \\t\\t\\tif err == nil || err == lease.ErrLeaseNotFound {\\n337: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn ttl, err\\n338: \\t\\t\\t}\\n339: \\t\\t}\\n340: \\t\\t// Throttle in case of e.g. connection problems.\\n341: \\t\\ttime.Sleep(50 * time.Millisecond)\\n342: \\t}\\n343: \\n344: \\tif cctx.Err() == context.DeadlineExceeded {\\n345: \\t\\treturn -1, ErrTimeout\\n346: \\t}\\n347: \\treturn -1, ErrCanceled\\n348: data)\\n791: \\tif err != nil {\\n792: \\t\\tproposalsFailed.Inc()\\n793: \\t\\ts.w.Trigger(id, nil) // GC wait\\n794: \\t\\treturn nil, err\\n795: \\t}\\n796: \\tproposalsPending.Inc()\\n797: \\t\\ts.w.Trigger(id, nil) // GC wait\\n805: \\t\\treturn nil, s.parseProposeCtxErr(cctx.Err(), start)\\n806: \\tcase <-s.done:\\n807: \\t\\treturn nil, ErrStopped\\n808: \\t}\\n809: }\\n810: \\n811: // Watchable returns a watchable interface attached to the etcdserver.\\n812: func (s *EtcdServer) Watchable() mvcc.WatchableKV { return s.KV() }\\n813: \\n814: func (s *EtcdServer) linearizableReadLoop() {\\n815: \\tfor {\\n816: \\t\\trequestId := s.reqIDGen.Next()\\n817: \\t\\tleaderChangedNotifier := s.LeaderChangedNotify()\\n818: \\t\\tselect {\\n819: \\t\\tcase ErrStopped\\n869: }\\n870: \\n871: func (s *EtcdServer) requestCurrentIndex(leaderChangedNotifier <-chan struct{}, requestId uint64) (uint64, error) {\\n872: \\terr := s.sendReadIndex(requestId)\\n873: \\tif err != nil {\\n874: \\t\\treturn 0, err\\n875: \\t}\\n876: \\n877: \\tlg := s.Logger()\\n878: \\terrorTimer := time.NewTimer(s.Cfg.ReqTimeout())\\n879: \\tdefer \\t\\t\\t\\t// continue waiting for the response of the current requests.\\n893: \\t\\t\\t\\tresponseId := uint64(0)\\n894: \\t\\t\\t\\tif len(rs.RequestCtx) == 8 {\\n895: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tresponseId = \\t\\t\\t\\t)\\n902: \\t\\t\\t\\tslowReadIndex.Inc()\\n903: \\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n904: \\t\\t\\t}\\n905: \\t\\t\\treturn rs.Index, nil\\n906: \\t\\tcase <-leaderChangedNotifier:\\n907: // Watchable returns a watchable interface attached to the etcdserver.\\n812: func (s *EtcdServer) Watchable() mvcc.WatchableKV { return s.KV() }\\n813: \\n814: func (s *EtcdServer) linearizableReadLoop() {\\n815: \\tfor {\\n816: \\t\\trequestId := s.reqIDGen.Next()\\n817: \\t\\tleaderChangedNotifier := s.LeaderChangedNotify()\\n818: \\t\\tselect {\\n819: \\t\\tcase \\t\\t\\t}\\n858: \\t\\t}\\n859: \\t\\t// unblock all l-reads requested at indices before confirmedIndex\\n860: \\t\\tnr.notify(nil)\\n861: \\t\\ttrace.Step(\\\"applied index is now lower than readState.Index\\\")\\n862: \\n863: \\t\\ttrace.LogAllStepsIfLong(traceThreshold)\\n864: \\t}\\n865: }\\n866: \\n867: func isStopped(err error) bool {\\n868: \\treturn err == raft.ErrStopped || err == ErrStopped\\n869: }\\n870: \\n871: func (s *EtcdServer) requestCurrentIndex(leaderChangedNotifier <-chan struct{}, requestId uint64) (uint64, error) {\\n872: \\terr := s.sendReadIndex(requestId)\\n873: \\tif err != nil {\\n874: \\t\\treturn 0, err\\n875: \\t}\\n876: \\n877: \\tlg := s.Logger()\\n878: \\terrorTimer := time.NewTimer(s.Cfg.ReqTimeout())\\n879: \\tdefer errorTimer.Stop()\\n880: \\tretryTimer := time.NewTimer(readIndexRetryTime)\\n881: \\tdefer retryTimer.Stop()\\n882: \\n883: \\tfirstCommitInTermNotifier := s.FirstCommitInTermNotify()\\n884: \\n885: \\t\\t\\t\\t// continue waiting for the response of the current requests.\\n893: \\t\\t\\t\\tresponseId := uint64(0)\\n894: \\t\\t\\t\\tif len(rs.RequestCtx) == 8 {\\n895: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tresponseId = \\t\\t\\t\\t)\\n902: \\t\\t\\t\\tslowReadIndex.Inc()\\n903: \\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n904: \\t\\t\\t}\\n905: \\t\\t\\treturn rs.Index, nil\\n906: \\t\\tcase <-leaderChangedNotifier:\\n907: (s *EtcdServer) sendReadIndex(requestIndex uint64) error {\\n951: \\tctxToSend := uint64ToBigEndianBytes(requestIndex)\\n952: \\n953: \\tcctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), *pb.CompactionRequest) (*pb.CompactionResponse, error) {\n225: \tstartTime := time.Now()\n226: \tresult, err := s.processInternalRaftRequestOnce(ctx, pb.InternalRaftRequest{Compaction: r})\n227: \ttrace := traceutil.TODO()\n228: \tif result != nil && result.trace != nil {\n229: \t\ttrace = result.trace\n230: \t\tdefer func() {\n231: \t\t\ttrace.LogIfLong(traceThreshold)\n232: \t\t}()\n233: \t\tapplyStart := result.trace.GetStartTime()\n234: \t\tresult.trace.SetStartTime(startTime)\n235: \t\ttrace.InsertStep(0, applyStart, \"process raft request\")\n236: \t}\n237: \tif r.Physical && result != nil && result.physc != nil {\n238: \t\t<-result.physc\n239: \t\t// The compaction is done deleting keys; the hash is now settled\n240: \t\t// but the data is not necessarily committed. If there's a crash,\n241: \t\t// the hash may revert to a hash prior to compaction completing\n242: \t\t// if the compaction resumes. Force the finished compaction to\n243: \t\t// commit so it won't resume following a crash.\n244: \t\ts.be.ForceCommit()\n245: \t\ttrace.Step(\"physically apply compaction\")\n246: \t}\n247: \tif err != nil {\n248: \t\treturn nil, err\n249: \t}\n250: \tif result.err != nil {\n251: \t\treturn nil, result.err\n252: \t}\n253: \tresp := result.resp.(*pb.CompactionResponse)\n254: \tif resp == nil {\n255: \t\tresp = &pb.CompactionResponse{}\n256: \t}\n257: \tif resp.Header == nil {\n258: \t\tresp.Header = &pb.ResponseHeader{}\n259: \t}\n260: \tresp.Header.Revision = s.kv.Rev()\n261: \ttrace.AddField(traceutil.Field{Key: \"response_revision\", Value: resp.Header.Revision})\n262: \treturn resp, nil\n263: }\n264: \n265: func (s *EtcdServer) LeaseGrant(ctx context.Context, r *pb.LeaseGrantRequest) (*pb.LeaseGrantResponse, error) {\n266: \t// no id given? choose one\n267: \tfor r.ID == int64(lease.NoLease) {\n268: \t\t// only use positive int64 id's\n269: \t\tr.ID = int64(s.reqIDGen.Next() & ((1 << 63) - 1))\n270: \t}\n271: \tresp, err := s.raftRequestOnce(ctx, pb.InternalRaftRequest{LeaseGrant: r})\n272: \tif err != nil {\n273: \t\treturn nil, err\n274: \t}\n275: \treturn resp.(*pb.LeaseGrantResponse), nil\n276: }\n277: \n278: func (s *EtcdServer) waitAppliedIndex() error {\n279: \tselect {\n280: \tcase <-s.ApplyWait():\n281: \tcase <-s.stopping:\n282: \t\treturn ErrStopped\n283: \tcase <-time.After(applyTimeout):\n284: \t\treturn ErrTimeoutWaitAppliedIndex\n285: \t}\n286: \n287: \treturn nil\n288: }\n289: \n290: func (s *EtcdServer) LeaseRevoke(ctx context.Context, r *pb.LeaseRevokeRequest) (*pb.LeaseRevokeResponse, error) {\n291: \tresp, err := s.raftRequestOnce(ctx, pb.InternalRaftRequest{LeaseRevoke: r})\n292: \tif err != nil {\n293: \t\treturn nil, err\n294: \t}\n295: \treturn resp.(*pb.LeaseRevokeResponse), nil\n296: }\n297: \n298: func (s *EtcdServer) LeaseRenew(ctx context.Context, id lease.LeaseID) (int64, error) {\n299: \tif s.isLeader() {\n300: \t\t// If s.isLeader() returns true, but we fail to ensure the current\n301: \t\t// member's leadership, there are a couple of possibilities:\n302: \t\t//   1. current member gets stuck on writing WAL entries;\n303: \t\t//   2. current member is in network isolation status;\n304: \t\t//   3. current member isn't a leader anymore (possibly due to #1 above).\n305: \t\t// In such case, we just return error to client, so that the client can\n306: \t\t// switch to another member to continue the lease keep-alive operation.\n307: \t\tif !s.ensureLeadership() {\n308: \t\t\treturn -1, lease.ErrNotPrimary\n309: \t\t}\n310: \n311: \t\tif err := s.waitAppliedIndex(); err != nil {\n312: \t\t\treturn 0, err\n313: \t\t}\n314: \n315: \t\tttl, err := s.lessor.Renew(id)\n316: \t\tif err == nil { // already requested to primary lessor(leader)\n317: \t\t\treturn ttl, nil\n318: \t\t}\n319: \t\tif err != lease.ErrNotPrimary {\n320: \t\t\treturn -1, err\n321: \t\t}\n322: \t}\n323: \n324: \tcctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, s.Cfg.ReqTimeout())\n325: \tdefer cancel()\n326: \n327: \t// renewals don't go through raft; forward to leader manually\n328: \tfor cctx.Err() == nil {\n329: \t\tleader, lerr := s.waitLeader(cctx)\n330: \t\tif lerr != nil {\n331: \t\t\treturn -1, lerr\n332: \t\t}\n333: \t\tfor _, url := range leader.PeerURLs {\n334: \t\t\tlurl := url + leasehttp.LeasePrefix\n335: \t\t\tttl, err := leasehttp.RenewHTTP(cctx, id, lurl, s.peerRt)\n336: \t\t\tif err == nil || err == lease.ErrLeaseNotFound {\n337: \t\t\t\treturn ttl, err\n338: \t\t\t}\n339: \t\t}\n340: \t\t// Throttle in case of e.g. connection problems.\n341: \t\ttime.Sleep(50 * time.Millisecond)\n342: \t}\n343: \n344: \tif cctx.Err() == context.DeadlineExceeded {\n345: \t\treturn -1, ErrTimeout\n346: \t}\n347: \treturn -1, ErrCanceled\n348: }\n349: \n350: func (s *EtcdServer) checkLeaseTimeToLive(ctx context.Context, leaseID lease.LeaseID) (uint64, error) {\n351: \trev := s.AuthStore().Revision()\n352: \tif !s.AuthStore().IsAuthEnabled() {\n353: \t\treturn rev, nil\n354: \t}\n355: \tauthInfo, err := s.AuthInfoFromCtx(ctx)\n356: \tif err != nil {\n357: \t\treturn rev, err\n358: \t}\n359: \tif authInfo == nil {\n360: \t\treturn rev, auth.ErrUserEmpty\n361: \t}\n362: \n363: \tl := s.lessor.Lookup(leaseID)\n364: \tif l != nil {\n365: \t\tfor _, key := range l.Keys() {\n366: \t\t\tif err := s.AuthStore().IsRangePermitted(authInfo, []byte(key), []byte{}); err != nil {\n367: \t\t\t\treturn 0, err\n368: \t\t\t}\n369: \t\t}\n370: \t}\n371: \n372: \treturn rev, nil\n373: }\n374: \n375: func (s *EtcdServer) leaseTimeToLive(ctx context.Context, r *pb.LeaseTimeToLiveRequest) (*pb.LeaseTimeToLiveResponse, error) {\n376: \tif s.isLeader() {\n377: \t\tif err := s.waitAppliedIndex(); err != nil {\n378: \t\t\treturn nil, err\n379: \t\t}\n380: \n381: \t\t// gofail: var beforeLookupWhenLeaseTimeToLive struct{}\n382: \n383: \t\t// primary; timetolive directly from leader\n384: \t\tle := s.lessor.Lookup(lease.LeaseID(r.ID))\n385: \t\tif le == nil {\n386: \t\t\treturn nil, lease.ErrLeaseNotFound\n387: \t\t}\n388: \t\t// TODO: fill out ResponseHeader\n389: \t\tresp := &pb.LeaseTimeToLiveResponse{Header: &pb.ResponseHeader{}, ID: r.ID, TTL: int64(le.Remaining().Seconds()), GrantedTTL: le.TTL()}\n390: \t\tif r.Keys {\n391: \t\t\tks := le.Keys()\n392: \t\t\tkbs := make([][]byte, len(ks))\n393: \t\t\tfor i := range ks {\n394: \t\t\t\tkbs[i] = []byte(ks[i])\n395: \t\t\t}\n396: \t\t\tresp.Keys = kbs\n397: \t\t}\n398: \n399: \t\t// The leasor could be demoted if leader changed during lookup.\n400: \t\t// We should return error to force retry instead of returning\n401: \t\t// incorrect remaining TTL.\n402: \t\tif le.Demoted() {\n403: \t\t\t// NOTE: lease.ErrNotPrimary is not retryable error for\n404: \t\t\t// client. Instead, uses ErrLeaderChanged.\n405: \t\t\treturn nil, ErrLeaderChanged\n406: \t\t}\n407: \t\treturn resp, nil\n408: \t}\n409: \n410: \tcctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, s.Cfg.ReqTimeout())\n411: \tdefer cancel()\n412: \n413: \t// forward to leader\n414: \tfor cctx.Err() == nil {\n415: \t\tleader, err := s.waitLeader(cctx)\n416: \t\tif err != nil {\n417: \t\t\treturn nil, err\n418: \t\t}\n419: \t\tfor _, url := range leader.PeerURLs {\n420: \t\t\tlurl := url + leasehttp.LeaseInternalPrefix\n421: \t\t\tresp, err := leasehttp.TimeToLiveHTTP(cctx, lease.LeaseID(r.ID), r.Keys, lurl, s.peerRt)\n422: \t\t\tif err == nil {\n423: \t\t\t\treturn resp.LeaseTimeToLiveResponse, nil\n424: \t\t\t}\n425: \t\t\tif err == lease.ErrLeaseNotFound {\n426: \t\t\t\treturn nil, err\n427: \t\t\t}\n428: \t\t}\n429: \t}\n430: \n431: \tif cctx.Err() == context.DeadlineExceeded {\n432: \t\treturn nil, ErrTimeout\n433: \t}\n434: \treturn nil, ErrCanceled\n435: }\n436: \n437: func (s *EtcdServer) LeaseTimeToLive(ctx context.Context, r *pb.LeaseTimeToLiveRequest) (*pb.LeaseTimeToLiveResponse, error) {\n438: \tvar rev uint64\n439: \tvar err error\n440: \tif \n523: func (s *EtcdServer) Authenticate(ctx context.Context, r *pb.AuthenticateRequest) (*pb.AuthenticateResponse, error) {\n524: \tif err := s.linearizableReadNotify(ctx); err != nil {\n525: \t\treturn nil, err\n526: \t}\n527: \n528: \tlg := s.Logger()\n529: \n530: \t// fix https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2021-28235\n531: \tdefer func() {\n532: \t\tif r != nil {\n533: }\n698: \n699: func (s *EtcdServer) raftRequestOnce(ctx context.Context, r pb.InternalRaftRequest) (proto.Message, error) {\n700: \tresult, err := s.processInternalRaftRequestOnce(ctx, r)\n701: \tif err != nil {\n702: \t\treturn nil, err\n703: \t}\n704: \tif result.err != nil {\n705: \t\treturn nil, result.err\n706: \t}\n707: \tif startTime, ok := ctx.Value(traceutil.StartTimeKey).(time.Time); ok && result.trace != nil {\n708: \t\tapplyStart := result.trace.GetStartTime()\n709: \t\t// The trace object is created in apply. Here reset the start time to trace\n710: \t\t// the raft request time by the difference between the request start time\n711: \t\t// and apply start time\n712: \t\tresult.trace.SetStartTime(startTime)\n713: \t\tresult.trace.InsertStep(0, applyStart, \"process raft request\")\n714: \t\tresult.trace.LogIfLong(traceThreshold)\n715: \t}\n716: \treturn result.resp, nil\n717: }\n718: \n719: func (s *EtcdServer) raftRequest(ctx context.Context, r pb.InternalRaftRequest) (proto.Message, error) {\n720: \treturn s.raftRequestOnce(ctx, r)\n721: }\n722: \n723: // doSerialize handles the auth logic, with permissions checked by \"chk\", for a serialized request \"get\". Returns a non-nil error on authentication failure.\n724: func (s *EtcdServer) doSerialize(ctx context.Context, chk func(*auth.AuthInfo) error, get func()) error {\n725: \ttrace := traceutil.Get(ctx)\n726: \tai, err := s.AuthInfoFromCtx(ctx)\n727: \tif err != nil {\n728: \t\treturn err\n729: \t}\n730: \tif ai == nil {\n731: \t\t// chk expects non-nil AuthInfo; use empty credentials\n732: \t\tai = &auth.AuthInfo{}\n733: \t}\n734: \tif err = chk(ai); err != nil {\n735: \t\treturn err\n736: \t}\n737: \ttrace.Step(\"get authentication metadata\")\n738: \t// fetch response for serialized request\n739: \tget()\n740: \t// check for stale token revision in case the auth store was updated while\n741: \t// the request has been handled.\n742: \tif ai.Revision != 0 && ai.Revision != s.authStore.Revision() {\n743: \t\treturn auth.ErrAuthOldRevision\n744: \t}\n745: \treturn nil\n746: }\n747: \n748: func (s *EtcdServer) processInternalRaftRequestOnce(ctx context.Context, r pb.InternalRaftRequest) (*applyResult, error) {\n749: \tai := s.getAppliedIndex()\n750: \tci := s.getCommittedIndex()\n751: \tif ci > ai+maxGapBetweenApplyAndCommitIndex {\n752: \t\treturn nil, ErrTooManyRequests\n753: \t}\n754: \n755: \tr.Header = &pb.RequestHeader{\n756: \t\tID: s.reqIDGen.Next(),\n757: \t}\n758: \n759: \t// check authinfo if it is not InternalAuthenticateRequest\n760: \tif r.Authenticate == nil {\n761: \t\tauthInfo, err := s.AuthInfoFromCtx(ctx)\n762: \t\tif err != nil {\n763: \t\t\treturn nil, err\n764: \t\t}\n765: \t\tif authInfo != nil {\n766: \t\t\tr.Header.Username = authInfo.Username\n767: \t\t\tr.Header.AuthRevision = authInfo.Revision\n768: \t\t}\n769: \t}\n770: \n771: \tdata, err := r.Marshal()\n772: \tif err != nil {\n773: \t\treturn nil, err\n774: \t}\n775: \n776: \tif len(data) > int(s.Cfg.MaxRequestBytes) {\n777: \t\treturn nil, ErrRequestTooLarge\n778: \t}\n779: \n780: \tid := r.ID\n781: \tif id == 0 {\n782: \t\tid = r.Header.ID\n783: \t}\n784: \tch := s.w.Register(id)\n785: \n786: \tcctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, s.Cfg.ReqTimeout())\n787: \tdefer cancel()\n788: \n789: \tstart := time.Now()\n790: \terr = s.r.Propose(cctx, data)\n791: \tif err != nil {\n792: \t\tproposalsFailed.Inc()\n793: \t\ts.w.Trigger(id, nil) // GC wait\n794: \t\treturn nil, err\n795: \t}\n796: \tproposalsPending.Inc()\n797: \tdefer proposalsPending.Dec()\n798: \n799: \tselect {\n800: \tcase x := <-ch:\n801: \t\treturn x.(*applyResult), nil\n802: \tcase <-cctx.Done():\n803: \t\tproposalsFailed.Inc()\n804: \t\ts.w.Trigger(id, nil) // GC wait\n805: \t\treturn nil, s.parseProposeCtxErr(cctx.Err(), start)\n806: \tcase <-s.done:\n807: \t\treturn nil, ErrStopped\n808: \t}\n809: }\n810: \n811: // Watchable returns a watchable interface attached to the etcdserver.\n812: func (s *EtcdServer) Watchable() mvcc.WatchableKV { return s.KV() }\n813: \n814: func (s *EtcdServer) linearizableReadLoop() {\n815: \tfor {\n816: \t\trequestId := s.reqIDGen.Next()\n817: \t\tleaderChangedNotifier := s.LeaderChangedNotify()\n818: \t\tselect {\n819: \t\tcase <-leaderChangedNotifier:\n820: \t\t\tcontinue\n821: \t\tcase <-s.readwaitc:\n822: \t\tcase <-s.stopping:\n823: \t\t\treturn\n824: \t\t}\n825: \n826: \t\t// as a single loop is can unlock multiple reads, it is not very useful\n827: \t\t// to propagate the trace from Txn or Range.\n828: \t\ttrace := traceutil.New(\"linearizableReadLoop\", s.Logger())\n829: \n830: \t\tnextnr := newNotifier()\n831: \t\ts.readMu.Lock()\n832: \t\tnr := s.readNotifier\n833: \t\ts.readNotifier = nextnr\n834: \t\ts.readMu.Unlock()\n835: \n836: \t\tconfirmedIndex, err := s.requestCurrentIndex(leaderChangedNotifier, requestId)\n837: \t\tif isStopped(err) {\n838: \t\t\treturn\n839: \t\t}\n840: \t\tif err != nil {\n841: \t\t\tnr.notify(err)\n842: \t\t\tcontinue\n843: \t\t}\n844: \n845: \t\ttrace.Step(\"read index received\")\n846: \n847: \t\ttrace.AddField(traceutil.Field{Key: \"readStateIndex\", Value: confirmedIndex})\n848: \n849: \t\tappliedIndex := s.getAppliedIndex()\n850: \t\ttrace.AddField(traceutil.Field{Key: \"appliedIndex\", Value: strconv.FormatUint(appliedIndex, 10)})\n851: \n852: \t\tif appliedIndex < confirmedIndex {\n853: \t\t\tselect {\n854: \t\t\tcase <-s.applyWait.Wait(confirmedIndex):\n855: \t\t\tcase <-s.stopping:\n856: \t\t\t\treturn\n857: \t\t\t}\n858: \t\t}\n859: \t\t// unblock all l-reads requested at indices before confirmedIndex\n860: \t\tnr.notify(nil)\n861: \t\ttrace.Step(\"applied index is now lower than readState.Index\")\n862: \n863: \t\ttrace.LogAllStepsIfLong(traceThreshold)\n864: \t}\n865: }\n866: \n867: func isStopped(err error) bool {\n868: \treturn err == raft.ErrStopped || err == ErrStopped\n869: }\n870: \n871: func (s *EtcdServer) requestCurrentIndex(leaderChangedNotifier <-chan struct{}, requestId uint64) (uint64, error) {\n872: \terr := s.sendReadIndex(requestId)\n873: \tif err != nil {\n874: \t\treturn 0, err\n875: \t}\n876: \n877: \tlg := s.Logger()\n878: \terrorTimer := time.NewTimer(s.Cfg.ReqTimeout())\n879: \tdefer errorTimer.Stop()\n880: \tretryTimer := time.NewTimer(readIndexRetryTime)\n881: \tdefer retryTimer.Stop()\n882: \n883: \tfirstCommitInTermNotifier := s.FirstCommitInTermNotify()\n884: \n885: \tfor {\n886: \t\tselect {\n887: \t\tcase rs := <-s.r.readStateC:\n888: \t\t\trequestIdBytes := uint64ToBigEndianBytes(requestId)\n889: \t\t\tgotOwnResponse := bytes.Equal(rs.RequestCtx, requestIdBytes)\n890: \t\t\tif !gotOwnResponse {\n891: \t\t\t\t// a previous request might time out. now we should ignore the response of it and\n892: \t\t\t\t// continue waiting for the response of the current requests.\n893: \t\t\t\tresponseId := uint64(0)\n894: \t\t\t\tif len(rs.RequestCtx) == 8 {\n895: \t\t\t\t\tresponseId = binary.BigEndian.Uint64(rs.RequestCtx)\n896: \t\t\t\t}\n897: \t\t\t\tlg.Warn(\n898: \t\t\t\t\t\"ignored out-of-date read index response; local node read indexes queueing up and waiting to be in sync with leader\",\n899: \t\t\t\t\tzap.Uint64(\"sent-request-id\", requestId),\n900: \t\t\t\t\tzap.Uint64(\"received-request-id\", responseId),\n901: \t\t\t\t)\n902: \t\t\t\tslowReadIndex.Inc()\n903: \t\t\t\tcontinue\n904: \t\t\t}\n905: \t\t\treturn rs.Index, nil\n906: \t\tcase <-leaderChangedNotifier:\n907: \t\t\treadIndexFailed.Inc()\n908: \t\t\t// return a retryable error.\n909: \t\t\treturn 0, ErrLeaderChanged\n910: \t\tcase <-firstCommitInTermNotifier:\n911: \t\t\tfirstCommitInTermNotifier = s.FirstCommitInTermNotify()\n912: \t\t\tlg.Info(\"first commit in current term: resending ReadIndex request\")\n913: \t\t\terr := s.sendReadIndex(requestId)\n914: \t\t\tif err != nil {\n915: \t\t\t\treturn 0, err\n916: \t\t\t}\n917: \t\t\tretryTimer.Reset(readIndexRetryTime)\n918: \t\t\tcontinue\n919: \t\tcase <-retryTimer.C:\n920: \t\t\tlg.Warn(\n921: \t\t\t\t\"waiting for ReadIndex response took too long, retrying\",\n922: \t\t\t\tzap.Uint64(\"sent-request-id\", requestId),\n923: \t\t\t\tzap.Duration(\"retry-timeout\", readIndexRetryTime),\n924: \t\t\t)\n925: \t\t\terr := s.sendReadIndex(requestId)\n926: \t\t\tif err != nil {\n927: \t\t\t\treturn 0, err\n928: \t\t\t}\n929: \t\t\tretryTimer.Reset(readIndexRetryTime)\n930: \t\t\tcontinue\n931: \t\tcase <-errorTimer.C:\n932: \t\t\tlg.Warn(\n933: \t\t\t\t\"timed out waiting for read index response (local node might have slow network)\",\n934: \t\t\t\tzap.Duration(\"timeout\", s.Cfg.ReqTimeout()),\n935: \t\t\t)\n936: \t\t\tslowReadIndex.Inc()\n937: \t\t\treturn 0, ErrTimeout\n938: \t\tcase <-s.stopping:\n939: \t\t\treturn 0, ErrStopped\n940: \t\t}\n941: \t}\n942: }\n943: \n944: func uint64ToBigEndianBytes(number uint64) []byte {\n945: \tbyteResult := make([]byte, 8)\n946: \tbinary.BigEndian.PutUint64(byteResult, number)\n947: \treturn byteResult\n948: }\n949: \n950: func (s *EtcdServer) sendReadIndex(requestIndex uint64) error {\n951: \tctxToSend := uint64ToBigEndianBytes(requestIndex)\n952: \n953: \tcctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), s.Cfg.ReqTimeout())\n954: \terr := s.r.ReadIndex(cctx, ctxToSend)\n955: \tcancel()\n956: \t\treadIndexFailed.Inc()\n963: \t\treturn err\n964: \t}\n965: \treturn nil\n966: }\n967: \n968: func (s *EtcdServer) LinearizableReadNotify(ctx context.Context) error {\n969: \treturn \n983: \t// wait for read state notification\n984: \tselect {\n985: \tcase <-nc.c:\n986: \t\treturn nc.err\n987: \tcase <-ctx.Done():\n988: \t\treturn ctx.Err()\n989: \tcase <-s.done:\n990: \t\treturn In `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/create.go:53-237`, the `createHandler` function processes the request:\\n\\n1. Line 102-113: Parses CreateOptions from query parameters\\n2. The key is formed as `/kubernetes.io/deployments/namespaces/{namespace}/name/{name}` and the value is the encoded Deployment protobuf.\\n\\n### Layer 4: etcd Storage Backend (etcd-io/etcd)\\n\\nThe kubernetes API server uses etcd as its distributed key-value store backend. The store sends a Put request to etcd via the gRPC v3 API.\\n\\nIn `server/etcdserver/api/v3rpc/key.go:27-39`, the `kvServer` struct receives the Put request at the RPC layer:\\n- `NewKVServer(s *etcdserver.EtcdServer)` creates the server (line 37-39)\\n- `kvServer.Put(ctx context.Context, r *pb.PutRequest)` handles incoming Put requests (lines 55-67)\\n\\nThe kvServer delegates to the underlying etcd server:\\n```go\\nresp, err := s.kv.Put(ctx, r)  // line 60\\n```\\n\\nThis calls `EtcdServer.Put()` in `server/etcdserver/v3_server.go:139-146`:\\n\\n```go\\nfunc (s *EtcdServer) Put(ctx context.Context, r *pb.PutRequest) (*pb.PutResponse, error) {\\n    ctx = context.WithValue(ctx, traceutil.StartTimeKey, time.Now())\\n    resp, err := s.raftRequest(ctx, pb.InternalRaftRequest{Put: r})\\n    if err != nil {\\n        return nil, err\\n    }\\n    return resp.(*pb.PutResponse), nil\\n}\\n```\\n\\nThe Put request is wrapped in an InternalRaftRequest and sent to the Raft consensus engine via `s.raftRequest()` (line 141).\\n\\nThe request is:\\n1. Proposed to the Raft cluster for consensus (raftRequest → processInternalRaftRequestOnce → s.r.Propose)\\n2. Replicated across the cluster members\\n3. Applied to the MVCC key-value store once committed\\n4. Persisted to the bbolt embedded BoltDB backend\\n\\nThe `RaftKV` interface (lines 54-60) defines the contract that the EtcdServer implements for Put operations, ensuring the Deployment is durably stored in the distributed etcd cluster.\\n\\n### Summary of the Call Chain\\n\\n1. **Client**: `deploymentsClient.Create()` → `rest.Post().Body().Do(ctx)` → HTTP POST to API server\\n2. **API Server**: `handlers.CreateResource()` → `handlers.createHandler()` → `REST.Create()`\\n3. **Storage**: `genericregistry.Store.Create()` → encodes object and calls etcd backend\\n4. **etcd**: `kvServer.Put()` → `EtcdServer.Put()` → `raftRequest()` → applies to MVCC store → persists to bbolt backend\\n\\nThis design ensures the Deployment object is:\\n- Validated and admitted at the API server level\\n- Stored durably in etcd with consensus guarantees\\n- Accessible to controllers and watchers immediately after creation\\n- Recoverable in case of failures due to Raft replication\"\n}\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":11,"cache_creation_input_tokens":17713,"cache_read_input_tokens":106329,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":17713},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"e5e5c475-ac89-4c5d-84c7-66219e5f0af5","uuid":"d567188d-a40c-4b47-8a7f-3cc2e9c7a0e3"}

End-to-End Trace\\n\\n### Layer 1: Client Library (kubernetes-client-go)\\n\\nWhen a user creates a Deployment, they call:\\n```go\\ndeploymentsClient.Create(ctx, deployment, metav1.CreateOptions{})\\n```\\n\\nThe `deploymentsClient` is of type `deployments` (kubernetes/typed/apps/v1/deployment.go:66), which implements the `DeploymentInterface` interface. This struct embeds `*gentype.ClientWithListAndApply` which provides the actual Create method implementation.\\n\\nThe Create method constructs an HTTP POST request using the kubernetes-client-go REST client. In `rest/client.go:215`, the `RESTClient.Post()` method returns a new REST request that will POST to the endpoint `/apis/apps/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/deployments`. The request body contains the serialized Deployment object and is sent via `request.Do(ctx)` which executes the HTTP request against the API server.\\n\\n### Layer 2: API Server Handler (kubernetes)\\n\\nThe Kubernetes API server receives the POST request and routes it to the REST handler. In `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/create.go:53-237`, the `createHandler` function processes the request:\\n\\n1. Line 102-113: Parses CreateOptions from query parameters\\n2. The REST struct embeds `*genericregistry.Store` which implements the actual Create operation.\\n\\n### Layer 3: Storage Handler (kubernetes)\\n\\nThe `REST.Create()` method (inherited from `genericregistry.Store`) in `pkg/registry/apps/deployment/storage/storage.go:92-116` sets up the storage configuration:\\n\\n1. The key is formed as `/kubernetes.io/deployments/namespaces/{namespace}/name/{name}` and the value is the encoded Deployment protobuf.\\n\\n### Layer 4: etcd Storage Backend (etcd-io/etcd)\\n\\nThe kubernetes API server uses etcd as its distributed key-value store backend. The store sends a Put request to etcd via the gRPC v3 API.\\n\\nIn `server/etcdserver/api/v3rpc/key.go:27-39`, the `kvServer` struct receives the Put request at the RPC layer:\\n- `NewKVServer(s *etcdserver.EtcdServer)` creates the server (line 37-39)\\n- `kvServer.Put(ctx context.Context, r *pb.PutRequest)` handles incoming Put requests (lines 55-67)\\n\\nThe kvServer delegates to the underlying etcd server:\\n```go\\nresp, err := s.kv.Put(ctx, r)  // line 60\\n```\\n\\nThis calls `EtcdServer.Put()` in `server/etcdserver/v3_server.go:139-146`:\\n\\n```go\\nfunc (s *EtcdServer) Put(ctx context.Context, r *pb.PutRequest) (*pb.PutResponse, error) {\\n    ctx = context.WithValue(ctx, traceutil.StartTimeKey, time.Now())\\n    resp, err := s.raftRequest(ctx, pb.InternalRaftRequest{Put: r})\\n    if err != nil {\\n        return nil, err\\n    }\\n    return resp.(*pb.PutResponse), nil\\n}\\n```\\n\\nThe Put request is wrapped in an InternalRaftRequest and sent to the Raft consensus engine via `s.raftRequest()` (line 141).\\n\\nThe request is:\\n1. Proposed to the Raft cluster for consensus (raftRequest → processInternalRaftRequestOnce → s.r.Propose)\\n2. Replicated across the cluster members\\n3. Applied to the MVCC key-value store once committed\\n4. Persisted to the bbolt embedded BoltDB backend\\n\\nThe `RaftKV` interface (lines 54-60) defines the contract that the EtcdServer implements for Put operations, ensuring the Deployment is durably stored in the distributed etcd cluster.\\n\\n### Summary of the Call Chain\\n\\n1. **Client**: `deploymentsClient.Create()` → `rest.Post().Body().Do(ctx)` → HTTP POST to API server\\n2. **API Server**: `handlers.CreateResource()` → `handlers.createHandler()` → `REST.Create()`\\n3. **Storage**: `genericregistry.Store.Create()` → encodes object and calls etcd backend\\n4. **etcd**: `kvServer.Put()` → `EtcdServer.Put()` → `raftRequest()` → applies to MVCC store → persists to bbolt backend\\n\\nThis design ensures the Deployment object is:\\n- Validated and admitted at the API server level\\n- Stored durably in etcd with consensus guarantees\\n- Accessible to controllers and watchers immediately after creation\\n- Recoverable in case of failures due to Raft replication\"\n}\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null}}
